prompt:
A small electronics factory produces two types of components: Alpha (α) and Beta (β).
- Component α requires 10 minutes to assemble and uses 2 units of raw material X.
- Component β requires 15 minutes to assemble and uses 3 units of raw material X.
Raw material X costs $5 per unit.

The factory typically operates 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. They have a weekly standing order to produce 100 Alpha components and 80 Beta components.

In a specific "surge" week, the factory operates for an extended total of 50 working hours. They prioritize fulfilling the entire weekly standing order first. After the standing order is completed, they use all remaining available production time in that week to produce *only* additional Alpha components. They also have an initial stock of 1000 units of raw material X at the start of this surge week.

What is the total cost of raw material X used during this specific "surge" week, assuming they don't purchase any new material during the week and only use from their initial stock?
